message.content:
You are the SIMILARITY AUDITOR (both-sides reviewer) in the clean-room process — read
/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md. Apply abstraction-filtration-comparison: identical
output strings / observed behavior / interface-doc names are NOT violations (compatibility content);
FLAG internal decomposition mirroring the Haskell where alternatives exist, non-observable internal
names matching Haskell internals, echoed comments, or algorithmic expression matching where the
observed behavior admits materially different implementations. Cross-check suspicious identical
strings against the cluster's QUERIES.log/BEHAVIOR.md — observed-output provenance filters them.
Append findings to the cluster's AUDIT.md under the specified heading (per finding: clean file:line,
Haskell file:line, why it survives filtration, a BEHAVIORAL redo instruction). Return a plain-text
verdict per cluster ("<name>: pass|redo") with one-paragraph justification and finding counts.
No structured output. Do not modify any other files.
Four NEW clusters, first audits (create AUDIT.md in each cluster dir, heading "## Round 1 audit"):
1. /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/ vs HASKELL src/Main/{Console.hs,
   Environment.hs} and src/Main.hs + the modes under src/Main/Mode/ (arg tables, help text assembly).
2. /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/macros/workspace/macro-clean/ vs HASKELL lib/term/src/Term/Macro.hs,
   lib/theory macro handling in TheoryObject.hs, and Theory/Text/Parser/Macro.hs.
3. /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/injective/workspace/injfacts-clean/ vs HASKELL
   lib/theory/src/Theory/Tools/InjectiveFactInstances.hs. Key: the clean decision rule was derived
   from live probes (QUERIES.log batches 1-6) — check whether the Haskell mechanism matches only in
   behavior or also in expression.
4. /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/derivcheck/workspace/derivcheck-clean/ vs HASKELL
   lib/theory/src/Theory/Tools/MessageDerivationChecks.hs. Key: the callback-trait architecture must
   NOT mirror the Haskell's probe-theory construction expression; verify probe payloads are
   behaviorally motivated.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).
47

1	# Unit D: CLI/console surface
2	
3	Build crate cli-clean in workspace/ reproducing the tamarin-prover BINARY's
4	command-line text surface byte-for-byte, from probing oracle/hs_oracle.sh
5	(passes arbitrary args to the black-box binary; examples in oracle/examples/).
6	Cover: --help (global and per-mode; enumerate modes from help output),
7	--version, unrecognized flag/argument error messages, missing/unreadable file
8	errors, the batch-mode output FRAMING around a processed theory (banner,
9	"analyzed: ..." summary block, timing/appendix lines — treat the theory
10	pretty-print itself as an opaque payload slot), and flag parsing semantics
11	(which flags take values, defaults shown in help). Deliverable: parse(argv)
12	-> structured command | error-text, plus render_help/render_version/framing
13	functions. Own design; no interface header exists. BEHAVIOR.md + QUERIES.log
14	+ tests with captured fixtures; REPORT.md + 10-line summary.
15

1	# BEHAVIOR.md — Unit D: CLI/console text surface
2	
3	Every statement below traces to an oracle observation captured under
4	`workspace/captures/` (file names in `[brackets]`) or to the SPEC. No
5	tamarin-prover internals were read; all behavior derived from black-box probing
6	of `oracle/hs_oracle.sh`.
7	
8	Oracle note: `hs_oracle.sh <arg1> [rest...]` consumes `arg1` as the first
9	token then appends the rest, i.e. it runs `tamarin-prover <arg1> <rest...>`
10	with stderr merged into stdout. A true zero-argument invocation is therefore
11	NOT observable through this oracle (it always passes at least one token); the
12	closest observable is a single empty-string token (see Ambiguous mode).
13	
14	## 1. Top-level shape
15	
16	The binary is a multi-mode command (cmdargs-style). The first token selects a
17	**mode** by unique prefix match against the mode names; otherwise the default
18	(batch) mode is used and the token is treated as an input FILE.
19	
20	Modes enumerated from help output `[help_global]`:
21	- (default / no token) = batch mode — positional `FILES`.
22	- `interactive` — "Start a web-server to construct proofs interactively." — positional `WORKDIR`.
23	- `variants` — "Compute the variants of the intruder rules for DH-exponentiation." — no positional.
24	- `test` — "Self-test the tamarin-prover installation." — positional `FILES`.
25	
26	Mode prefix matching `[mode_prefix_int/_v/_t]`: `int`→interactive, `v`→variants,
27	`t`→test (any unambiguous non-empty prefix works). An empty token matches all
28	three → ambiguous `[noargs]`.
29	
30	A flag appearing BEFORE a would-be mode token keeps the default batch mode; the
31	token then becomes a filename `[flag_before_mode]`: `-v interactive` →
32	default mode, "interactive" opened as a file → openFile error.
33	
34	## 2. Exit codes
35	
36	- `--help` / per-mode `--help`, `--version`/`-V`, successful batch run: exit 0.
37	- Every parse error and every runtime error observed: exit 1.
38	
39	## 3. `--help` (global and per-mode)  [help_global, help_interactive, help_variants, help_test]
40	
41	Each mode's help has the same skeleton:
42	
43	    <mode-usage-line>
44	      <mode-description>.
45	
46	    Commands:
47	      interactive  Start a web-server to construct proofs interactively.
48	      variants     Compute the variants of the intruder rules for DH-exponentiation.
49	      test         Self-test the tamarin-prover installation.
50	
51	    Flags:
52	      <flag rows, two-column, right column wrapped to a fixed width>
53	    About:
54	      <help/version rows>
55	
56	    <blank>
57	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
58	    To show help for different commands, type tamarin-prover [Command] --help.
59	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	    See 'https://github.com/tamarin-prover/tamarin-prover/blob/master/README.md'
61	    for usage instructions and pointers to examples.
62	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	    <blank>
64	
65	Usage lines:
66	- global: `tamarin-prover [COMMAND] ... [OPTIONS] FILES`
67	- interactive: `interactive [COMMAND] ... [OPTIONS] WORKDIR`
68	- variants: `variants [COMMAND] ... [OPTIONS]`   (no positional)
69	- test: `test [COMMAND] ... [OPTIONS] FILES`
70	
71	The Commands block is IDENTICAL in all four helps. The footer block (7 lines
72	ending with a trailing blank line) is identical in all four. Every help output
73	ends with `----\n\n` (footer rule + one trailing empty line).
74	
75	The right-hand description column width differs by mode because cmdargs sizes
76	the left column to the widest flag in THAT mode: global uses a very wide left
77	column (widest flag `-m --output-module[=...]`), the other three narrower.
78	Because the exact wrapping/padding is compatibility output, the four help texts
79	are stored verbatim as fixtures and emitted as opaque strings — the clean code
80	does not re-derive column math.
81	
82	Availability of About rows:
83	- default/global mode: `-? --help` AND `-V --version`.
84	- interactive / variants / test: only `-? --help` (no `--version`). Confirmed:
85	  `variants --version` → `Unknown flag: --version` `[version_from_mode]`.
86	
87	### Flag inventory & value semantics (from help text)
88	
89	Value markers: `[=X]` = value OPTIONAL; `=FILE` (no brackets) = value shown
90	required (but see note). Defaults appear in the description text.
91	
92	Default/batch mode flags:
93	- `--prove[=LEMMAPREFIX*|LEMMANAME]`     optional value, repeatable
94	- `--lemma[=LEMMAPREFIX*|LEMMANAME]`     optional value, repeatable
95	- `--stop-on-trace[=DFS|BFS|SEQDFS|SORRY|NONE]`  optional, default DFS
96	- `-b --bound[=INT]`                     optional
97	- `--heuristic[=(C|I|O|P|S|c|i|o|p|s|{.})+]`  optional, default 's'
98	- `--partial-evaluation[=SUMMARY|VERBOSE]`   optional
99	- `-D --defines[=STRING]`                optional
100	- `--diff`                               no value
101	- `--quit-on-warning`                    no value
102	- `--auto-sources`                       no value
103	- `--oraclename[=FILE]`                  optional; default './theory_filename.oracle', fallback './oracle'
104	- `--oracle-only`                        no value
105	- `--quiet`                              no value
106	- `-v --verbose`                         no value
107	- `-c --open-chains[=PositiveInteger]`   optional, default 10
108	- `-s --saturation[=PositiveInteger]`    optional, default 5
109	- `-d --derivcheck-timeout[=INT]`        optional, default 5 (0 deactivates)
110	- `--no-reuse`                           no value
111	- `--no-restrictions`                    no value
112	- `--replication-bound[=INT]`            optional
113	- `--no-compress`                        no value
114	- `--parse-only`                         no value
115	- `--precompute-only`                    no value
116	- `-o --output[=FILE]`                   optional
117	- `-O --Output[=DIR]`                    optional
118	- `-m --output-module[=spthytyped|spthy|msr|proverifequiv|proverif|deepsec]`  optional enum
119	- `--output-json=FILE` (alias `--oj`)    value shown required; NO parse error when omitted [oj_alias_noval,outputjson_noval]
120	- `--output-dot=FILE`  (alias `--od`)    value shown required
121	- `--with-dot[=FILE]`                    optional
122	- `--with-json[=FILE]`                   optional
123	- `--with-maude[=FILE]`                  optional
124	- About: `-? --help`, `-V --version`
125	
126	interactive mode adds: `-p --port[=PORT]`, `-i --interface[=INTERFACE]`,
127	`--image-format[=PNG|SVG]` (default SVG), `--debug`, `--no-logging`; plus the
128	proving/output subset (--prove … --with-maude minus the file-output-module
129	flags: no `--no-compress/--parse-only/--precompute-only/-o/-O/-m/--output-json/
130	--output-dot`). About: only `-? --help`.
131	
132	variants mode: only `-O --Output[=DIR]`; About `-? --help`.
133	
134	test mode: only `--with-dot/--with-json/--with-maude`; About `-? --help`.
135	
136	## 4. `--version` and `-V`  [version, short_version]
137	
138	`--version` and `-V` produce identical output (only valid in default mode).
139	The maude readiness check runs concurrently and its lines INTERLEAVE with the
140	version banner in a fixed, reproducible order:
141	
142	    maude tool: '<MAUDE_PATH>'
143	     checking version: <BANNER line 1: tamarin-prover <VER>, (C) <authors>, <YEARS>>
144	    <blank>
145	    This program comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY. It is free software, and you
146	    are welcome to redistribute it according to its LICENSE, see
147	    'https://github.com/tamarin-prover/tamarin-prover/blob/master/LICENSE'.
148	    <blank>
149	    <MAUDE_VER>. OK.
150	     checking installation: OK.
151	    Generated from:
152	    Tamarin version <VER>
153	    Maude version <MAUDE_VER>
154	    Git revision: <GITREV> (with uncommited changes), branch: <BRANCH>
155	    Compiled at: <TIMESTAMP>
156	
157	The banner text (` checking version: ` prefix + tamarin line + warranty block)
158	is emitted between the maude check's "checking version:" and its "<ver>. OK."
159	because the maude subprocess is being awaited. Dynamic/environment slots:
160	`<MAUDE_PATH>` (default 'maude'), `<VER>` (observed 1.13.0), `<YEARS>`
161	(2010-2023), `<MAUDE_VER>` (3.5.1), `<GITREV>` + dirty-suffix + `<BRANCH>`,
162	`<TIMESTAMP>`. These are build/runtime metadata, injected into the template.
163	
164	## 5. maude preamble (framing prelude)
165	
166	Batch/variants runs that CLOSE a theory (need maude) print a 3-line prelude
167	`[batch_nslpk3_default]`:
168	
169	    maude tool: '<MAUDE_PATH>'
170	     checking version: <MAUDE_VER>. OK.
171	     checking installation: OK.
172	
173	`--parse-only` does NOT print the preamble `[batch_nslpk3_parseonly]` (parsing
174	needs no maude). Runtime file-open errors are printed AFTER this preamble.
175	
176	## 6. Batch-mode framing around a processed theory
177	
178	Full default run `[batch_nslpk3_default]`:
179	
180	    <maude preamble (3 lines)>
181	    [Theory <NAME>] Theory loaded
182	    [Theory <NAME>] Theory translated
183	    [Theory <NAME>] Derivation checks started
184	    [Theory <NAME>] Derivation checks ended
185	    [Theory <NAME>] Theory closed
186	    <OPAQUE THEORY PAYLOAD, from `theory <NAME>` through `end`>
187	    <blank>
188	    ==============================================================================
189	    summary of summaries:
190	    <blank>
191	    analyzed: <ABSOLUTE_FILE_PATH>
192	    <blank>
193	      processing time: <T>s
194	      <-- literal 2-space line
195	    <RESULT LINES>
196	    <blank>
197	    ==============================================================================
198	
199	- `====` rule is exactly 78 `=`. Output ends `====\n` (no trailing content).
200	- `--prove` uses the SAME 5 progress phases `[batch_nslpk3_prove]`.
201	- `--parse-only` prints ONLY `[Theory <NAME>] Theory loaded`, then payload; NO
202	  preamble, NO summary block `[batch_nslpk3_parseonly]`.
203	- The "Generated from:" version comment and any `WARNING:` block are part of the
204	  OPAQUE theory payload (inside `theory … end`), not framing.
205	
206	RESULT LINE forms (2-space indent):
207	- `  <lemma> (all-traces): analysis incomplete (<N> steps)`  [default, unselected]
208	- `  <lemma> (all-traces): verified (<N> steps)`             [proved true]  `[batch_nslpk3_prove]`
209	- `  <lemma> (exists-trace): <result> (<N> steps)`           [exists-trace kind]
210	- (form `falsified (<N> steps)` inferred as the counterpart to `verified`; not
211	  directly captured — see LIMITATIONS.)
212	- `  WARNING: <N> wellformedness check failed!`  [when checks failed; note the
213	  singular "check"] `[round2_multiplication_in_rule_lhs]`
214	
215	When a theory has warnings but no lemmas, the WARNING line is the sole result
216	line. Ordering of the WARNING line vs lemma lines when BOTH are present is not
217	observed (LIMITATIONS).
218	
219	`<T>` (processing time) and `<ABSOLUTE_FILE_PATH>` are dynamic slots.
220	
221	## 7. variants mode run  [mode_variants_run]
222	
223	`variants` (no file): maude preamble, then the pretty-printed intruder
224	variant rules (opaque payload), NO summary block.
225	
226	## 8. Error text taxonomy
227	
228	### 8a. cmdargs parse errors — bare one-liner, exit 1, no help:
229	- Unknown long flag `[err_unknown_flag]`:  `Unknown flag: --foobar`
230	- Unknown short flag `[err_unknown_short]`: `Unknown flag: -Z` (note: `-h` is
231	  NOT help; help is `-?`/`--help`) `[short_help]`
232	- Value given to a valueless flag `[help_eq]`: `Unhandled argument to flag, none expected: --help=foo`
233	- Ambiguous mode `[noargs]`: `Ambiguous mode '', could be any of: interactive variants test`
234	
235	### 8b. cmdargs validation errors — message + blank + FULL help of the active mode, exit 1:
236	- No input file (flags otherwise valid) `[err_stopontrace_bad, err_noinput_diff, err_noinput_prove]`:
237	      error: no input files given
238	      <blank>
239	      <global help body>
240	  Envelope = `error: no input files given\n\n` + exact global help.
241	- Bad interactive WORKDIR `[err_two_modes, interactive_two_pos]`:
242	      error: directory '<DIR>' does not exist.
243	      <blank>
244	      <interactive help body>
245	  (`interactive test` → 'test' is the WORKDIR; `interactive aa bb` → names 'bb'.)
246	
247	### 8c. runtime errors — printed AFTER the maude preamble, exit 1:
248	File open (IOException, no CallStack):
249	- missing `[err_missing_file, token_notfile]`:  `tamarin-prover: <path>: openFile: does not exist (No such file or directory)`
250	- unreadable `[err_unreadable]`:                 `tamarin-prover: <path>: openFile: permission denied (Permission denied)`
251	- directory `[err_isdir]`:                       `tamarin-prover: <path>: openFile: inappropriate type (is a directory)`
252	
253	Application `error` with Haskell CallStack (all cite the same call site):
254	- bad bound `[err_bound_nonint]`:
255	      tamarin-prover: bound: invalid bound given
256	      CallStack (from HasCallStack):
257	        error, called at src/Main/Mode/Batch.hs:162:33 in main:Main.Mode.Batch
258	- bad output module `[err_output_module_bad]`:
259	      tamarin-prover: output mode not supported.
260	      CallStack (from HasCallStack):
261	        error, called at src/Main/Mode/Batch.hs:162:33 in main:Main.Mode.Batch
262	
263	These runtime strings (incl. the CallStack source path) are compatibility
264	output taken verbatim from the oracle; they are emitted by the runtime layer
265	after parse(), so the clean crate exposes them as render helpers rather than
266	from parse().
267	
268	## 9. LIMITATIONS / unobserved (documented, not guessed in code)
269	- True zero-argument behavior: not observable through this oracle.
270	- `falsified (<N> steps)` exact string: inferred counterpart of `verified`; not
271	  directly captured (no small example produced it within probe budget).
272	- WARNING-line vs lemma-line ordering in the summary when both present.
273	- Whether `--output-json`/`--output-dot` error at output time when value omitted
274	  (only parse-time / --parse-only observed, where no error occurs).
275	- Additional per-proof progress lines under heavy `--prove` (none seen on NSLPK3).
276

1	//! Modes and their flag tables.
2	//!
3	//! The binary is a multi-mode command. The first token selects a mode by unique
4	//! prefix match against the mode names; otherwise the default (batch) mode is
5	//! used and the token is a positional. Each mode recognizes a different set of
6	//! flags, so the flag table is mode-scoped (a flag valid in one mode is
7	//! "Unknown" in another — e.g. `--version` outside the default mode).
8	
9	/// The four invocation modes.
10	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
11	pub enum Mode {
12	    /// Default mode when no mode token is given: analyze/prove theory FILES.
13	    Batch,
14	    /// `interactive` — web server; positional WORKDIR.
15	    Interactive,
16	    /// `variants` — compute intruder-rule variants; no positional.
17	    Variants,
18	    /// `test` — self-test; positional FILES.
19	    Test,
20	}
21	
22	impl Mode {
23	    /// The three named modes, in the order the help/ambiguity messages list them.
24	    pub const NAMED: [(&'static str, Mode); 3] = [
25	        ("interactive", Mode::Interactive),
26	        ("variants", Mode::Variants),
27	        ("test", Mode::Test),
28	    ];
29	
30	    /// Modes whose named token is a prefix of `token` (empty token matches all).
31	    pub fn prefix_matches(token: &str) -> Vec<(&'static str, Mode)> {
32	        Mode::NAMED
33	            .iter()
34	            .filter(|(name, _)| name.starts_with(token))
35	            .copied()
36	            .collect()
37	    }
38	}
39	
40	/// One recognized flag: its canonical long name, optional short char, whether it
41	/// accepts a value, and any additional long aliases.
42	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
43	pub struct FlagSpec {
44	    pub long: &'static str,
45	    pub short: Option<char>,
46	    pub takes_value: bool,
47	    pub aliases: &'static [&'static str],
48	}
49	
50	const fn boolf(long: &'static str, short: Option<char>) -> FlagSpec {
51	    FlagSpec { long, short, takes_value: false, aliases: &[] }
52	}
53	const fn valf(long: &'static str, short: Option<char>) -> FlagSpec {
54	    FlagSpec { long, short, takes_value: true, aliases: &[] }
55	}
56	
57	// The proving/output flag subset shared by the default and interactive modes.
58	const PROVE_COMMON: &[FlagSpec] = &[
59	    valf("prove", None),
60	    valf("lemma", None),
61	    valf("stop-on-trace", None),
62	    valf("bound", Some('b')),
63	    valf("heuristic", None),
64	    valf("partial-evaluation", None),
65	    valf("defines", Some('D')),
66	    boolf("diff", None),
67	    boolf("quit-on-warning", None),
68	    boolf("auto-sources", None),
69	    valf("oraclename", None),
70	    boolf("oracle-only", None),
71	    boolf("quiet", None),
72	    boolf("verbose", Some('v')),
73	    valf("open-chains", Some('c')),
74	    valf("saturation", Some('s')),
75	    valf("derivcheck-timeout", Some('d')),
76	    boolf("no-reuse", None),
77	    boolf("no-restrictions", None),
78	    valf("replication-bound", None),
79	];
80	
81	const WITH_TOOLS: &[FlagSpec] = &[
82	    valf("with-dot", None),
83	    valf("with-json", None),
84	    valf("with-maude", None),
85	];
86	
87	/// Flags recognized in the default/batch mode (excluding About flags).
88	pub fn batch_flags() -> Vec<FlagSpec> {
89	    let mut v: Vec<FlagSpec> = PROVE_COMMON.to_vec();
90	    v.extend_from_slice(&[
91	        boolf("no-compress", None),
92	        boolf("parse-only", None),
93	        boolf("precompute-only", None),
94	        valf("output", Some('o')),
95	        valf("Output", Some('O')),
96	        valf("output-module", Some('m')),
97	        FlagSpec { long: "output-json", short: None, takes_value: true, aliases: &["oj"] },
98	        FlagSpec { long: "output-dot", short: None, takes_value: true, aliases: &["od"] },
99	    ]);
100	    v.extend_from_slice(WITH_TOOLS);
101	    v
102	}
103	
104	/// Flags recognized in the interactive mode (excluding About flags).
105	pub fn interactive_flags() -> Vec<FlagSpec> {
106	    let mut v = vec![
107	        valf("port", Some('p')),
108	        valf("interface", Some('i')),
109	        valf("image-format", None),
110	        boolf("debug", None),
111	        boolf("no-logging", None),
112	    ];
113	    v.extend_from_slice(PROVE_COMMON);
114	    v.extend_from_slice(WITH_TOOLS);
115	    v
116	}
117	
118	/// Flags recognized in the variants mode (excluding About flags).
119	pub fn variants_flags() -> Vec<FlagSpec> {
120	    vec![valf("Output", Some('O'))]
121	}
122	
123	/// Flags recognized in the test mode (excluding About flags).
124	pub fn test_flags() -> Vec<FlagSpec> {
125	    WITH_TOOLS.to_vec()
126	}
127	
128	/// The mode-scoped flag table (mode flags first, then About flags). `--version`
129	/// / `-V` is an About flag only in the default mode.
130	pub fn flags_for(mode: Mode) -> Vec<FlagSpec> {
131	    let mut v = match mode {
132	        Mode::Batch => batch_flags(),
133	        Mode::Interactive => interactive_flags(),
134	        Mode::Variants => variants_flags(),
135	        Mode::Test => test_flags(),
136	    };
137	    // About: help everywhere; version only in the default mode.
138	    v.push(FlagSpec { long: "help", short: Some('?'), takes_value: false, aliases: &[] });
139	    if mode == Mode::Batch {
140	        v.push(boolf("version", Some('V')));
141	    }
142	    v
143	}

1	//! Version banner and the maude readiness preamble.
2	//!
3	//! In `--version`/`-V` output the maude readiness check and the version banner
4	//! interleave in a fixed, reproducible order (the banner is printed while the
5	//! maude subprocess is awaited). The observed byte layout is stored as a template
6	//! with `{{SLOT}}` markers for the build/runtime metadata; `render_version` fills
7	//! the slots. The static text of the template is byte-exact observed output.
8	
9	const VERSION_TEMPLATE: &str = include_str!("../fixtures/version.tmpl");
10	
11	/// Maude tool identity used in the readiness preamble.
12	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
13	pub struct MaudeInfo {
14	    /// Tool path as printed inside the quotes (default `maude`).
15	    pub path: String,
16	    /// Reported maude version, e.g. `3.5.1`.
17	    pub version: String,
18	}
19	
20	impl Default for MaudeInfo {
21	    fn default() -> Self {
22	        MaudeInfo { path: "maude".to_string(), version: "3.5.1".to_string() }
23	    }
24	}
25	
26	/// Build/runtime metadata that fills the version banner slots.
27	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
28	pub struct VersionInfo {
29	    pub maude: MaudeInfo,
30	    pub tamarin_version: String,
31	    pub copyright_years: String,
32	    /// Everything after `Git revision: ` (revision + dirty note + branch).
33	    pub git_description: String,
34	    /// The `Compiled at:` timestamp text.
35	    pub compiled_at: String,
36	}
37	
38	/// The version banner, filling every slot in the observed template.
39	pub fn render_version(info: &VersionInfo) -> String {
40	    VERSION_TEMPLATE
41	        .replace("{{MAUDE_PATH}}", &info.maude.path)
42	        .replace("{{TAMARIN_VERSION}}", &info.tamarin_version)
43	        .replace("{{COPYRIGHT_YEARS}}", &info.copyright_years)
44	        .replace("{{MAUDE_VERSION}}", &info.maude.version)
45	        .replace("{{GIT_DESCRIPTION}}", &info.git_description)
46	        .replace("{{COMPILED_AT}}", &info.compiled_at)
47	}
48	
49	/// The 3-line maude readiness preamble emitted before batch/variants processing
50	/// (each line terminated by a newline).
51	pub fn maude_preamble(maude: &MaudeInfo) -> String {
52	    format!(
53	        "maude tool: '{}'\n checking version: {}. OK.\n checking installation: OK.\n",
54	        maude.path, maude.version
55	    )
56	}

80	-- Maude version functions - previously in Environment.hs
81	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	
83	-- | Path to maude tool
84	maudePath :: Arguments -> FilePath
85	maudePath = fromMaybe "maude" . findArg "withMaude"
86	
87	getVersionIO :: String -> IO String
88	getVersionIO maudeVersion = do
89	  let tamarinVersion = showVersion version
90	  pure $ "Generated from:\nTamarin version " ++ tamarinVersion
91	         ++ "\nMaude version " ++ maudeVersion ++ gitVersion
92	         ++ "\n" ++ compileTime
93	
94	commandLine :: String -> [String] -> String
95	commandLine prog args = unwords $ prog : args
96	
97	testProcess
98	  :: (String -> String -> Either String String)
99	  -- ^ Analysis of stdout, stderr. Use 'Left' to report error.
100	  -> String         -- ^ Default error message to display to the user.
101	  -> String         -- ^ Test description to display.
102	  -> FilePath       -- ^ Process to start
103	  -> [String]       -- ^ Arguments
104	  -> String         -- ^ Stdin
105	  -> Bool           -- ^ Whether to ignore ExitFailure
106	  -> Bool           -- ^ Whether Maude is being tested - hard fail for exceptions on Maude.
107	  -> IO (Maybe String)    -- ^ String with the process output, if test was successful
108	testProcess check defaultMsg testName prog args inp ignoreExitCode maudeTest = do
109	  putStrErr testName
110	  hFlush stdout
111	  hFlush stderr
112	  handle handler $ do
113	    (exitCode, out, err) <- readProcessWithExitCode prog args inp
114	    let errMsg reason = do
115	          putStrErrLn reason
116	          putStrErrLn $ "Detailed results from testing '" ++ prog ++ "'"
117	          putStrErrLn $ " command: " ++ commandLine prog args
118	          putStrErrLn $ " stdin:   " ++ inp
119	          putStrErrLn $ " stdout:  " ++ out
120	          putStrErrLn $ " stderr:  " ++ err
121	          pure Nothing
122	
123	    let check' = case check out err of
124	                   Left msg  -> errMsg msg
125	                   Right msg -> do putStrErrLn msg
126	                                   pure (Just out)
127	
128	    if not ignoreExitCode then
129	      case exitCode of
130	        ExitFailure code -> errMsg $
131	          "failed with exit code " ++ show code ++ "\n\n" ++ defaultMsg
132	        ExitSuccess      -> check'
133	    else check'
134	
135	  where
136	    putStrErrLn = hPutStrLn stderr
137	    putStrErr = hPutStr stderr
138	
139	    handler :: IOException -> IO (Maybe String)
140	    handler exception = do
141	      putStrErrLn "caught exception while executing:"
142	      putStrErrLn $ commandLine prog args
143	      putStrErrLn $ "with input: " ++ inp
144	      putStrErrLn "Exception: "
145	      putStrErrLn $ "   " ++ show exception
146	      if maudeTest then
147	        error "Maude is not installed. Ensure Maude is available and on the path."
148	        else putStrErrLn ""
149	      pure Nothing
150	
151	ensureMaude :: Arguments -> IO (Bool, String)
152	ensureMaude as = do
153	  hPutStrLn stderr $ "maude tool: '" ++ maude ++ "'"
154	  t1 <- testProcess checkVersion errMsg' " checking version: " maude ["--version"] "" False True
155	  t2 <- testProcess checkInstall errMsg' " checking installation: "   maude [] "quit\n" False True
156	  (_, out, _) <- readProcessWithExitCode maude ["--version"] ""
157	  pure $
158	    if isNothing t1 || isNothing t2 then
159	      (False, if out == "" then "unknown version\n" else init out ++ " (unsupported)\n")
160	    else
161	      (True, out)
162	  where
163	    maude = maudePath as
164	    checkVersion out _
165	      | strip out `elem` supportedVersions = Right (strip out ++ ". OK.")
166	      | otherwise                          = Left  $ errMsg $
167	          " 'maude --version' returned unsupported version '" ++ strip out ++ "'"
168	
169	    strip = reverse . dropWhile isSpace . reverse
170	
171	    checkInstall _ []  = Right "OK."
172	    checkInstall _ err = Left  $ errMsg err
173	
174	    --  Maude versions prior to 2.7.1 are no longer supported,
175	    --  because the 'get variants' command is incompatible.
176	    supportedVersions = ["2.7.1", "3.0", "3.1", "3.2.1", "3.2.2", "3.3", "3.3.1", "3.4", "3.5", "3.5.1"]
177	
178	    errMsg' = errMsg $ "'" ++ maude ++ "' executable not found / does not work"
179	
180	    errMsg reason = unlines
181	      [ "WARNING:"
182	      , ""
183	      , reason
184	      , " Please install one of the following versions of Maude: " ++ intercalate ", " supportedVersions
185	      ]
186	
187	-- Maude Version
188	ensureMaudeAndGetVersion :: Arguments -> IO String
189	ensureMaudeAndGetVersion as = do
190	  -- Ensure Maude version and get Maude version
191	  (_, maudeVersion) <- ensureMaude as
192	  -- Get String for version and put it in the arguments __version__
193	  getVersionIO maudeVersion
194

196	-- Static constants for the tamarin-prover
197	------------------------------------------------------------------------------

200	gitVersion :: String
201	gitVersion = concat
202	  [ "Git revision: "
203	  , $(gitHash)
204	  , if $(gitDirty) then

206	    else ""

214

231	  , "'https://github.com/tamarin-prover/tamarin-prover/blob/master/LICENSE'."
232	  ]

234	-- | Line width to use.
235	lineWidth :: Int
236	lineWidth = 110
237	
238	shortLineWidth :: Int
239	shortLineWidth = 78
240	
241	
242	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
243	-- A simple generic representation of arguments
244	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
245	
246	-- | A name of an argument.
247	type ArgKey = String
248	
249	-- | A value of an argument.
250	type ArgVal = String
251	
252	-- | It is most convenient to view arguments just as 'String' based key-value
253	-- pairs. If there are multiple values for the same key, then the left-most
254	-- one is preferred.
255	type Arguments = [(ArgKey,ArgVal)]
256	
257	-- | Does an argument exist.
258	argExists :: String -> Arguments -> Bool
259	argExists a = isJust . findArg a
260	
261	-- | Find the value(s) corresponding to the given key.
262	findArg :: MonadPlus m => ArgKey -> Arguments -> m ArgVal
263	findArg a' as = msum [ pure v | (a,v) <- as, a == a' ]
264	
265	-- | Find the value corresponding to the given key. Throw an error if no value
266	-- exists.
267	getArg :: ArgKey -> Arguments -> ArgVal
268	getArg a =
269	  fromMaybe (error $ "getArg: argument '" ++ a ++ "' not found") . findArg a
270	
271	-- | Add an argument to the from of the list of arguments.
272	addArg :: ArgKey -> ArgVal -> Arguments -> Arguments
273	addArg a v = ((a,v):)
274	
275	-- | Add an argument with the empty string as the value.
276	addEmptyArg :: String -> Arguments -> Arguments
277	addEmptyArg a = addArg a ""
278	
279	-- | Update an argument.
280	updateArg :: ArgKey -> ArgVal -> Arguments -> Either a Arguments
281	updateArg a v = Right . addArg a v
282	
283	-- | Add the help flag.
284	helpFlag :: Flag Arguments
285	helpFlag = flagHelpSimple (addEmptyArg "help")
286	
287	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
288	
289	-- Modes for using the Tamarin prover
290	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
291	
292	-- | A representation of an interaction mode with the Tamarin prover.
293	data TamarinMode = TamarinMode
294	  { name        :: String
295	  , cmdArgsMode :: Mode Arguments
296	    -- ^ Run is given a reference to the mode. This enables changing the
297	    -- static information of a mode and keeping the same 'run' function.
298	    -- We use this for implementing the 'main' mode.
299	  , run         :: TamarinMode -> Arguments -> IO ()
300	  , isMainMode  :: Bool
301	  }
302	
303	-- | Smart constructor for a 'TamarinMode'.
304	tamarinMode :: String -> Help
305	            -> (Mode Arguments -> Mode Arguments) -- ^ Changes to default mode.
306	            -> (TamarinMode -> Arguments -> IO ())
307	            -> TamarinMode
308	tamarinMode name help adaptMode run0 = TamarinMode
309	  { name = name
310	  , cmdArgsMode = adaptMode $ Mode
311	      { modeGroupModes = toGroup []
312	      , modeNames      = [name]
313	      , modeValue      = []
314	      , modeCheck      = updateArg "mode" name
315	      , modeExpandAt   = False
316	      , modeReform     = const Nothing-- no reform possibility
317	      , modeHelp       = help
318	      , modeHelpSuffix = []
319	      , modeArgs       = ([], Nothing)   -- no positional arguments
320	      , modeGroupFlags = toGroup [] -- no flags
321	      }
322	  , run        = run
323	  , isMainMode = False
324	  }
325	  where
326	    run thisMode as
327	      | argExists "help"    as = helpAndExit thisMode Nothing
328	      | argExists "version" as = do putStrLn versionStr
329	                                    versionMaude <- ensureMaudeAndGetVersion as
330	                                    putStrLn versionMaude
331	      | otherwise              = run0 thisMode as
332	
333	-- | Disply help message of a tamarin mode and exit.
334	helpAndExit :: TamarinMode -> Maybe String -> IO ()
335	helpAndExit tmode mayMsg = do
336	  putStrLn $ showText (Wrap lineWidth)
337	           $ helpText header HelpFormatOne tmode.cmdArgsMode
338	  -- output example info
339	  putStrLn $ unlines
340	    [ separator
341	    , "To show help for different commands, type tamarin-prover [Command] --help."
342	    , separator
343	    , "See 'https://github.com/tamarin-prover/tamarin-prover/blob/master/README.md'"
344	    , "for usage instructions and pointers to examples."
345	    , separator
346	    ]
347	  end
348	  where
349	    separator = replicate shortLineWidth '-'
350	    (header, end) = case mayMsg of
351	        Nothing  -> ([], pure ())
352	        Just msg -> (["error: " ++ msg], exitFailure)
353	
354	-- | Main function.
355	defaultMain :: TamarinMode -> [TamarinMode] -> IO ()
356	defaultMain firstMode otherModes = do
357	  as <- processArgs mainMode.cmdArgsMode
358	  case findArg "mode" as of
359	    Nothing   -> error "defaultMain: impossible - mode not set"
360	    Just name -> headNote "defaultMain: impossible - no mode found" $ do
361	      tmode <- mainMode : (setGroupModes <$> otherModes)
362	      guard (tmode.name == name)
363	      pure $ tmode.run tmode as
364	  where
365	    mainMode = setGroupModes $ firstMode
366	      { name        = programName
367	      , cmdArgsMode = firstMode.cmdArgsMode
368	          { modeNames = [programName]
369	          , modeCheck      = updateArg "mode" programName
370	          , modeGroupFlags = firstMode.cmdArgsMode.modeGroupFlags
371	              { groupNamed =
372	                  [ ("About"
373	                    , [ helpFlag
374	                      , flagVersion (addEmptyArg "version")
375	                      ] )
376	                  ]
377	              }
378	          }
379	      , isMainMode = True
380	      }
381	
382	    setGroupModes m = m { cmdArgsMode = m.cmdArgsMode { modeGroupModes } }
383	    modeGroupModes = toGroup (map (.cmdArgsMode) otherModes)
384	
385	
386	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
387	-- Pretty printing
388	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
389	
390	-- | Render a pretty-printing document.
391	renderDoc :: PP.Doc -> String
392	renderDoc = PP.renderStyle (PP.defaultStyle { PP.lineLength = lineWidth })

88	run thisMode as
89	  | null inFiles = helpAndExit thisMode (Just "no input files given")

100	  | argExists "outModule" as = do

116	      let (docs, reps, times) = unzip3 $ fmap (\((d, r), t) -> (d, r, t)) resTimed

132	        mapM_ (putStrLn . renderDoc) docs
133	        putStrLn $ renderDoc $ ppSummary summary

140	                                    , summary
141	                                    , Pretty.text ""
142	                                    , Pretty.text $ replicate 78 '=' ]
143	
144	    ppRep (inFile, outFile, time, summary) =
145	      Pretty.vcat
146	        [ Pretty.text $ "analyzed: " ++ inFile
147	        , Pretty.text ""
148	        , Pretty.text ""
149	        , Pretty.nest 2 $ Pretty.vcat
150	          [ maybe Pretty.emptyDoc (\o -> Pretty.text $ "output:          " ++ o) outFile
151	          , maybe Pretty.emptyDoc (\t -> Pretty.text $ printf "processing time: %.2fs" (realToFrac t :: Double)) time
152	          , Pretty.text ""
153	          , summary
154	          ]
155	        ]
156	
157	    -- handles to arguments
158	    -----------------------
159	    inFiles = reverse $ findArg "inFile" as
160	
161	    thyLoadOptions = case mkTheoryLoadOptions as of
162	      Left (ArgumentError e) -> error e
163	      Right opts             -> opts
164	
165	    -- output generation
166	    --------------------
167	    writeOutput = argExists "outFile" as || argExists "outDir" as
168	
169	    mkOutPath :: FilePath  -- ^ Input file name.
170	              -> Maybe FilePath  -- ^ Output file name.
171	    mkOutPath inFile =
172	      do outFile <- findArg "outFile" as
173	         guard (outFile /= "")
174	         pure outFile
175	      <|>
176	      do outDir <- findArg "outDir" as
177	         pure $ mkAutoPath outDir (takeBaseName inFile)
178	
179	    -- automatically generate the filename for output
180	    mkAutoPath :: FilePath -> String -> FilePath
181	    mkAutoPath dir baseName
182	      | argExists "html" as = dir </> baseName
183	      | otherwise           = dir </> addExtension (baseName ++ "_analyzed") "spthy"
184	
185	    -- theory processing functions
186	    ------------------------------
187	
188	    processThy :: String -> FilePath -> IO (Pretty.Doc, Pretty.Doc)
189	    processThy versionData inFile = either handleError pure <=< runExceptT $ do
190	      srcThy <- liftIO $ readFile inFile
191	      thy    <- loadTheory thyLoadOptions srcThy inFile
192	
193	      let sig = either (._thySignature) (._diffThySignature) thy
194	      sig'   <- liftIO $ toSignatureWithMaude thyLoadOptions.maudePath sig
195	
196	      -- | Pretty print the theory as is without performing any checks.
197	      if thyLoadOptions.parseOnlyMode then
198	        pure $ (, Pretty.emptyDoc) $ either prettyOpenTheory prettyOpenDiffTheory thy
199	
200	      -- | Execute precomputation steps and print the partial deconstructions
201	      else if thyLoadOptions.precomputeOnlyMode then do
202	        (report, thy') <- closeTheory versionData thyLoadOptions sig' thy
203	        case thy' of
204	          Left thy'' -> do
205	            pure (ppWf report Pretty.$--$ prettyPrecomputation thy'', ppWf report)
206	          Right thy'' -> do
207	            pure (ppWf report Pretty.$--$ prettyDiffPrecomputation thy'', ppWf report)
208	    
209	      -- | Translate and check thoery based on specified output module.
210	      else if isTranslateOnlyMode then do
211	        (report, thy') <- translateAndCheckTheory versionData thyLoadOptions sig' thy
212	
213	        -- comment: Not sure why this was needed. It just duplicates all comments in the theory.
214	        -- let thy'' = bimap (modify thyItems (++ (TextItem <$> formalComments thy')))
215	        --                   (modify diffThyItems (++ (DiffTextItem <$> formalComments thy')))
216	        --                   thy'
217	
218	        (, ppWf report) <$> either (liftIO . prettyOpenTheoryByModule thyLoadOptions)
219	                                   (pure . prettyOpenDiffTheory)
220	                                   thy'
221	
222	      -- | Close and potentially prove theory.
223	      else do
224	        (report, thy') <- closeTheory versionData thyLoadOptions sig' thy
225	        _ <- liftIO $ bitraverse outputTraces (const $ return ()) thy'
226	
227	        pure $
228	          either (\t -> (prettyClosedTheory t,     ppWf report Pretty.$--$ prettyClosedSummary t))
229	                 (\d -> (prettyClosedDiffTheory d, ppWf report Pretty.$--$ prettyClosedDiffSummary d))
230	                 thy'
231	      where
232	        isTranslateOnlyMode = isJust thyLoadOptions.outputModule
233	
234	        handleError e@(ParserError _) = die $ show e
235	        handleError (WarningError report) = do
236	          putStrLn $ renderDoc $ Pretty.vcat $ [ Pretty.text ""
237	                                               , Pretty.text "WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!" ]
238	                                            ++ [ Pretty.text "" | not $ null report ]
239	                                            ++ [ prettyWfErrorReport report
240	                                               , Pretty.text "" ]
241	          die "quit-on-warning mode selected - aborting on wellformedness errors."
242	
243	        ppWf []  = Pretty.emptyDoc
244	        ppWf rep = Pretty.vcat $
245	          Pretty.text ("WARNING: " ++ show (length rep) ++ " wellformedness check failed!")
246	          : [ Pretty.text   "         The analysis results might be wrong!" | thyLoadOptions.proveMode ]
247	
248	        -- | Output any found traces of the analyzed theory in dot/JSON format if the corresponing command line option is set.
249	        -- The output is dumped into a single file per format. Multiple dot graphs are simply concatenated into a single file,
250	        -- while the JSON schema already allows for multiple graphs.
251	        outputTraces :: ClosedTheory -> IO ()
252	        outputTraces thy = do
253	            let graphOptions = defaultGraphOptions
254	                dotOptions = defaultDotOptions
255	                serializeDot (label, system) = D.showDot label $ dotSystemCompact graphOptions dotOptions system
256	                serializeJSON = sequentsToJSONPretty graphOptions
257	                labelledSystems = map (\(lemma, proof, system) ->
258	                  let label = traceOutputLabel graphOptions dotOptions lemma proof in
259	                  (label, system)) systemsWithMetadata
260	
261	            case findArg "traceDot" as of
262	              Nothing -> pure ()
263	              Just outfile ->
264	                let serialized = intercalate "\n" $ map serializeDot labelledSystems in
265	                writeFile outfile serialized
266	
267	            case findArg "traceJSON" as of
268	              Nothing -> pure ()
269	              Just outfile ->
270	                let serialized = serializeJSON labelledSystems in
271	                writeFile outfile serialized
272	          where
273	            -- | Collect all solved (i.e. a trace was found) systems of the theory along with their
274	            -- path in the proof and the lemma in which they appear in the given theory.
275	            systemsWithMetadata :: [(Lemma IncrementalProof, ProofPath, System)]
276	            systemsWithMetadata = do
277	              lemma <- getLemmas thy
278	              let proof = lemma._lProof
279	              [(lemma, proofPath, system) | (proofPath, system) <- proofSystems proof]
280	
281	            -- | Collect all solved (i.e. a trace was found) systems of the theory along with their
282	            -- path in the proof.
283	            proofSystems :: IncrementalProof -> [(ProofPath, System)]
284	            proofSystems (LNode (ProofStep (Finished Solved) (Just rootSystem)) _) =  [([], rootSystem)]
285	            proofSystems (LNode (ProofStep _ _) children) =  
286	              [(l : ls, system) | (l, subProof) <- M.toList children 
287	                                , (ls, system) <- proofSystems subProof ]
288	
289	            -- | Make a label for use in the trace output out of all relevant information for a constraint system.
290	            traceOutputLabel :: GraphOptions
291	                             -> DotOptions
292	                             -> Lemma IncrementalProof
293	                             -> ProofPath
294	                             -> String
295	            traceOutputLabel graphOptions dotOptions lemma proofPath =
296	              "trace_"
297	              ++ thy._thyName                              -- Name of the theory in which the constraint system appears.
298	              ++ "_"
299	              ++ traceLabelOptions graphOptions dotOptions -- Graph options are included in a short format.
300	              ++ "_"
301	              ++ lemma._lName                              -- Name of the lemma in which the constraint system appears.
302	              ++ intercalate "-" proofPath                 -- Path through the proof where the constraint system is located.
303	
304	            -- | Format the graph rendering options in a concise way.
305	            traceLabelOptions :: GraphOptions -> DotOptions -> String
306	            traceLabelOptions graphOptions dotOptions =
307	              let s1 = show graphOptions._goSimplificationLevel
308	                  s2 = if graphOptions._goShowAutoSource then "AS1" else "AS0"
309	                  s3 = if graphOptions._goClustering then "CL1" else "CL0"
310	                  s4 = if graphOptions._goAbbreviate then "A1" else "A0"
311	                  s5 = if graphOptions._goCompress then "C1" else "C0"
312	                  s6 = case dotOptions._doNodeStyle of
313	                         FullBoringNodes -> "NF"
314	                         CompactBoringNodes -> "NB"
315	              in
316	                intercalate "-" [s1, s2, s3, s4, s5, s6]

42	readOutputCommand as =

58	-- | Read the cpu info using a call to cat /proc/cpuinfo
59	getCpuModel :: IO String
60	getCpuModel =
61	  handle handler $ do
62	    (_, info, _) <- readProcessWithExitCode "cat" ["/proc/cpuinfo"] []
63	    pure $ maybe errMsg
64	             (("Linux running on an "++) . drop 2 . dropWhile (/=':'))
65	             (find (isPrefixOf "model name") $ lines info)
66	  where

71	-- | Ensure a suitable version of the Graphviz dot tool is installed.
72	ensureGraphVizDot :: Arguments -> IO (Maybe String)
73	ensureGraphVizDot as = do
74	  hPutStrLn stderr $ "GraphViz tool: '" ++ dot ++ "'"
75	  dotExists <- testProcess (check "graphviz" "") errMsg1 " checking version: " dot ["-V"] "" False False
76	  if isJust dotExists
77	     then testProcess (check "png" "OK.") errMsg2 " checking PNG support: " dot ["-T?"] "" True False
78	     else pure dotExists
79	  where
80	    dot = dotPath as
81	    check str okMsg _ err
82	      | str `isInfixOf` err' = Right msg
83	      | otherwise = Left "Error."
84	      where err' = map toLower err
85	            msg  = if null okMsg
86	                      then init err' ++ ". OK."
87	                      else okMsg
88	    errMsg1 = unlines
89	      [ "WARNING:"
90	      , ""
91	      , " The dot tool seems not to be provided by Graphviz."
92	      , " Graph generation might not work."
93	      , " Please download an official version from:"
94	      , "         http://www.graphviz.org/"
95	      ]

100	      , " Graph generation might not work."
101	      ]
102	
103	-- | Check whether a the graph rendering command supplied is pointing to an existing file
104	ensureGraphCommand :: Arguments -> IO (Maybe String)
105	ensureGraphCommand as = do
106	  hPutStrLn stderr $ "Graph rendering command: " ++ cmd
107	  testProcess check errMsg "Checking availablity ..." "which" [cmd] "" False False

110	    ocmd = readOutputCommand as
111	    check _ err
112	      | err == ""  = Right " OK."
113	      | otherwise  = Left  errMsg
114	    errMsg = unlines
115	      [ "Command not found" ]

73	    wdIsFile <- doesFileExist workDir0

127	    thyLoadOptions = case mkTheoryLoadOptions as of
128	      Left (ArgumentError e) -> error e
129	      Right opts             -> opts

131	    -- Port argument

148	        Just "png" -> PNG
149	        Nothing    -> SVG
150	        Just _     -> error "image-format must be one of PNG|SVG"
151	
152	    serverUrl port = "http://" ++ address ++ ":" ++ show port
153	      where
154	        address | interface `elem` ["*","*4","*6"] = "127.0.0.1"
155	                | otherwise                        = interface
156	
157	    runWarp port wapp =
158	      handle (\e -> err (e::IOException)) $
159	        Warp.runSettings
160	          (setHost (fromString interface) $ setPort port defaultSettings)
161	          wapp
162	
163	    err e = error $
164	      "Starting the webserver on "++interface++" failed: \n"
165	      ++ show e
166	      ++ "\nNote that you can use '--interface=\"*4\"' for binding to all interfaces."
167

24	-- | Self-test mode.
25	testMode :: TamarinMode
26	testMode = tamarinMode
27	  "test"
28	  ("Self-test the " ++ programName ++ " installation.")
29	  setupFlags
30	  run
31	  where
32	    setupFlags defaultMode = defaultMode
33	      { modeArgs       = ([], Just $ flagArg (updateArg "inFile") "FILES")
34	      , modeGroupFlags = Group
35	          { groupUnnamed = toolFlags
36	          , groupHidden  = []
37	          , groupNamed   = [("About", [helpFlag])]
38	          }
39	      }
40	
41	-- | Run the self-test.
42	run :: TamarinMode -> Arguments -> IO ()
43	run _thisMode as = do
44	  putStrLn $ "Self-testing the " ++ programName ++ " installation."
45	  nextTopic "Testing the availability of the required tools"
46	  (successMaude, _) <- ensureMaude as
47	
48	#ifndef NO_GUI
49	  putStrLn ""
50	  maybeSuccessGraphVizDot <- ensureGraphVizDot as
51	  let successGraphVizDot = isJust maybeSuccessGraphVizDot
52	#else
53	  let successGraphVizDot = True
54	#endif
55	
56	  {-- FIXME (SM): move test-suite into its own .cabal section.
57	   -- I've disabled this part when I've moved to embedding all data files to
58	   -- simplify packaging.
59	   -- (2015-04-13)
60	
61	  --------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	  nextTopic "Testing the parser on our examples"
63	  examplePath   <- getDataFileName "examples"
64	  let mkParseTest = Parser.testParseFile Nothing
65	  parseTests    <- Parser.testParseDirectory mkParseTest 2 examplePath
66	  successParser <- runUnitTest $ TestList parseTests
67	
68	  --------------------------------------------------------------------------
69	  nextTopic "Testing the prover on some of our examples"
70	
71	  let heuristic  = roundRobinHeuristic [SmartRanking False]
72	      autoProver = AutoProver heuristic Nothing CutDFS
73	      prover = Just ( maudePath as
74	                    , replaceSorryProver $ runAutoProver autoProver
75	                    )
76	      mkProverTest file = do
77	          fullFile <- getDataFileName file
78	          pure $ Parser.testParseFile prover fullFile
79	
80	  nslEx    <- mkProverTest "examples/classic/NSLPK3.spthy"
81	  loopEx   <- mkProverTest "examples/loops/Minimal_Loop_Example.spthy"
82	  diffieEx <- mkProverTest "examples/csf12/JKL_TS1_2008_KI.spthy"
83	
84	  successProver <- runUnitTest $ TestList [ nslEx, loopEx, diffieEx ]
85	  --}
86	
87	  --------------------------------------------------------------------------
88	  nextTopic "Testing the unification infrastructure"
89	  successTerm  <- runUnitTest =<< Term.tests (maudePath as)
90	
91	  --------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	  -- FIXME: Implement regression testing.
93	  --
94	
95	  nextTopic "TEST SUMMARY"
96	  let success = successMaude && successGraphVizDot && successTerm -- , successParser, successProver ]
97	  if success then do
98	    putStrLn "All tests successful."
99	    putStrLn $ "The " ++ programName ++ " should work as intended."
100	    putStrLn "\n           :-) happy proving (-:\n"
101	    exitSuccess
102	  else do
103	    putStrLn "\nWARNING: Some tests failed."
104	    putStrLn $ "The " ++ programName ++ " might NOT WORK AS INTENDED.\n"
105	    exitFailure
106	  where
107	    nextTopic msg = putStrLn $ "\n*** " ++ msg ++ " ***"
108	
109	    runUnitTest test = do
110	      Counts _ _ termErrs termFails <- runTestTT test
111	      let success = termErrs == 0 && termFails == 0
112	      pure success

23	import Main.Utils

25	
26	intruderMode :: TamarinMode
27	intruderMode = tamarinMode
28	  "variants"
29	  "Compute the variants of the intruder rules for DH-exponentiation."
30	  setupFlags
31	  run
32	  where
33	    setupFlags defaultMode = defaultMode
34	      { modeArgs       = ([], Nothing )  -- no positional arguments
35	      , modeGroupFlags = Group outputFlags [] [("About", [helpFlag])]
36	      }
37	
38	    outputFlags =
39	      [ flagOpt "" ["Output","O"] (updateArg "outDir") "DIR"  "Output directory"
40	      ]
41	
42	-- | Compute the intruder variants.
43	run :: TamarinMode -> Arguments -> IO ()
44	run _thisMode as = do
45	  _ <- ensureMaude as
46	  dhHnd <- startMaude (maudePath as) dhMaudeSig
47	  bpHnd <- startMaude (maudePath as) bpMaudeSig
48	  let dhRules = dhIntruderRules False `runReader` dhHnd
49	      bpRules = bpIntruderRules False `runReader` bpHnd
50	      dhS = renderDoc . prettyIntruderVariants $ dhRules
51	      bpS = renderDoc . prettyIntruderVariants $ bpRules
52	
53	  putStrLn (dhS++bpS)
54	  writeRules dhS bpS
55	  where
56	    -- output generation
57	    --------------------
58	
59	    writeRules dhS bpS = case findArg "outDir" as of
60	      Just outDir -> do
61	        writeFileWithDirs (outDir </> dhIntruderVariantsFile) dhS
62	        writeFileWithDirs (outDir </> bpIntruderVariantsFile) bpS
63	      Nothing     -> pure ()

17	import           Term.Substitution
18

20	import           Data.List                  (find)
21	
22	type Macro c v = (B.ByteString, [v], Term (Lit c v))
23	-- | Standard type for macros
24	type LNMacro = Macro Name LVar
25	-- | Type used to handle macros in formulas
26	type BNMacro = Macro Name (BVar LVar)
27	
28	-- | Change a Macro to a FunSym
29	macroToFunSym :: Macro c v -> FunSym
30	macroToFunSym (op, args, _) = NoEq (op, (length args, Private, Destructor))      
31	
32	-- | Apply and substitute a single macro
33	applyMacro :: IsConst c => IsVar v => FunSym -> [v] -> VTerm c v -> VTerm c v -> VTerm c v
34	applyMacro mc margs mout (viewTerm -> FApp f targs)
35	    | mc == f = apply (substFromList $ zip margs (map (applyMacro mc margs mout) targs)) mout
36	    | otherwise = fApp f $ map (applyMacro mc margs mout) targs
37	applyMacro _ _ _ t = t
38	
39	-- | Apply and substitute all the macros
40	applyMacros :: IsConst c => IsVar v => [Macro c v] -> VTerm c v -> VTerm c v
41	applyMacros macros term = 
42	    case viewTerm term of
43	    FApp f args -> 
44	        case findMatchingMacro f macros of
45	            Just (_, margs, mout) -> 
46	                let processedArgs = map (applyMacros macros) args
47	                    subst = substFromList $ zip margs processedArgs
48	                in applyMacros macros (apply subst mout)
49	            Nothing -> 
50	                fApp f $ map (applyMacros macros) args
51	    Lit l -> lit l
52	  where
53	    findMatchingMacro :: FunSym -> [Macro c v] -> Maybe (Macro c v)
54	    findMatchingMacro f = find (\m -> macroToFunSym m == f)
55	
56	lnMacroToBNMacro :: LNMacro -> BNMacro
57	lnMacroToBNMacro (op, args, out) = (op, map freeLNTerm args, freeTerm out)
58	
59	lnMacrosToBNMacros :: [LNMacro] -> [BNMacro]
60	lnMacrosToBNMacros = map lnMacroToBNMacro

29	macros :: Parser [LNMacro]
30	macros = do
31	    mcs <- symbol "macros" *> colon *> commaSep macro
32	    return mcs
33	    where
34	      macro = do
35	        op <- BC.pack <$> identifier
36	        when (BC.unpack op `elem` reservedBuiltins)
37	            $ error $ "`" ++ show op ++ "` is a reserved function name for builtins."
38	        args <- parens $ commaSep lvar
39	        unless (length args == length (nub args))
40	            $ error $ show op ++ " have two arguments with the same name."
41	        out <- equalSign *> msetterm False llit
42	        sign <- sig <$> getState
43	        let mc = (op, args, out)
44	        let k = length args
45	        case lookup op (S.toList (stFunSyms sign) ++ S.toList (macroNames sign)) of
46	            Just _ -> fail $ "Conflicting name for macro " ++ BC.unpack op
47	            _ -> do
48	                modifyStateSig $ addMacroSym (op,(k,Private,Destructor))
49	                return mc
50	
51	getMacroName :: LNMacro -> String
52	getMacroName (op, _, _) = BC.unpack op

11	--
12	-- Computate an under-approximation to the set of all facts with unique
13	-- instances, i.e., fact whose instances never occur more than once in a
14	-- state. We use this information to reason about protocols that exploit
15	-- exclusivity of linear facts.
16	module Theory.Tools.InjectiveFactInstances
17	  ( -- * Computing injective fact instances.
18	    MonotonicBehaviour (..),
19	    simpleInjectiveFactInstances,
20	  )
21	where
22	
23	import Control.Applicative (empty)
24	import Control.DeepSeq

26	import Data.Binary
27	import Data.Label as L
28	import Data.List

35	
36	-- import           Debug.Trace
37	
38	-- unspecified = there is no rule using this fact
39	-- unstable = increasing and decreasing or not at all related inputs and outputs
40	data MonotonicBehaviour = Constant | Increasing | Decreasing | StrictlyIncreasing | StrictlyDecreasing | Unstable | Unspecified
41	  deriving (Eq, Ord, Generic, NFData, Binary)
42	
43	instance Show MonotonicBehaviour where
44	  show Constant = "="
45	  show Increasing = "≤"
46	  show Decreasing = "≥"
47	  show StrictlyIncreasing = "<"
48	  show StrictlyDecreasing = ">"
49	  show Unstable = "."
50	  show Unspecified = "?"
51	
52	
53	-- | under-approximate monotonicity constraints from a formula.
54	-- For now, we only deal with a formula that corresponds to a subterm atom
55	-- If we want to express things like OR, we need to return a more refined value.
56	extractConstraints :: (Ord c, Ord b) => ProtoFormula syn s c b -> [(Term (Lit c b), Term (Lit c b))]
57	extractConstraints (Ato (Subterm t1 t2)) = [(bvarToLVar t1, bvarToLVar t2)]
58	    where
59	        bvarToLVar =
60	            fmapTerm (fmap (foldBVar boundError id))
61	        boundError _ = error "implementation error: bounded variable in top-level atom"
62	extractConstraints _ = []
63	
64	
65	-- | Compute a simple under-approximation to the set of facts with injective
66	-- instances. A fact-tag is has injective instances, if there is no state of
67	-- the protocol with more than one instance with the same term as a first
68	-- argument of the fact-tag.
69	--
70	-- We compute the under-approximation by checking that
71	-- (1) the fact-tag is linear
72	-- (2) for each occurrence of the fact-tag in a rule:
73	--     there is no other occurrence with the same first term and
74	--       (a) either there is a Fr-fact of the first term as a premise
75	--       (b) or there is exactly one fact-tag with the same first term in a premise
76	--
77	-- Additionally, we determine the term positions that are
78	-- - Constant
79	-- - Increasing/Decreasing according to `elemNotBelowReducible` and equality
80	-- - Strictly Increasing/Decreasing according to `elemNotBelowReducible`
81	--
82	-- We also try to detect these positions inside of tuples that occur at
83	-- the top-level of an injective fact. For instance,
84	-- if injective fact S is used like this: [S(~id, <a, b>)] --> [S(~id, <a, a>)]
85	-- position two of S is not constant but position 2.2 is.
86	
87	-- To detect this, one needs to compute the _shape_ of a term at position i for
88	-- each protocol rule (modulo E) by checking how it is pattern-matched inside of
89	-- each rule. A natural choice would be to represent this shape via a binary tree
90	-- that matches the tuples shape, however, we choose to implement an
91	-- under-approximation of this by only flattening tuples to the right.
92	-- E.g., <<a, b>, c> gets flattened into [<a, b>, c]. This allows us to
93	-- detect constant/increasing etc. positions for the most common use case:
94	-- a tuple that contains 'dynamic' state and is only expanded to the right.
95	-- I.e., [S(~id, <a, b>)] --> [S(~id, <a, b, c>)]. In this example, the current
96	-- implement can still detect that 'a' is constant, but not 'b'.
97	--
98	-- We exclude facts that are not copied in a rule, as they are already handled
99	-- properly by the naive backwards reasoning.
100	simpleInjectiveFactInstances :: FunSig -> [ProtoRuleE] -> S.Set (FactTag, [[MonotonicBehaviour]])
101	simpleInjectiveFactInstances reducible rules = S.fromList $ do
102	  tag <- M.keys candidates
103	  let resultTag = combineAll (map (getMaybeEqStrict tag) rules) tag
104	  case resultTag of
105	    Just behaviours -> return (tag, behaviours) -- remove the 0-position from eqPos as this is the ~fr
106	    Nothing -> empty
107	  where
108	    -- Flatten only the right-hand side of a tuple
109	    getPairTerms :: LNTerm -> [LNTerm]
110	    getPairTerms (viewTerm2 -> FPair t1 t2) = t1 : getPairTerms t2
111	    getPairTerms t = [t]
112	
113	    -- Compute the potentially injective facts and their _shape_.
114	    -- The shape `s` of a FactTag is a list of lists where the inner list
115	    -- `s[i]` describes the shape of the term at position `i+1` in the fact.
116	    -- The shape of a term is the right-handed flattening of it as computed
117	    -- by 'getPairTerms'. E.g., shape (Var x) = [x], shape (<t1, t2>) = [t1, t2]
118	    -- and shape (<<t1, t2>, t3>) = [<t1, t2>, t3].
119	    -- Intuitively, if `length shape' = n, then `shape' can be unfolded to the
120	    -- right n - 1 times.
121	    candidates :: M.Map FactTag [[MonotonicBehaviour]]
122	    candidates = M.fromListWith combineShapes $ do
123	      ru <- rules
124	      conc <- L.get rConcs ru
125	      let tag = factTag conc
126	      guard $
127	        (factTagMultiplicity tag == Linear)
128	          && (tag `elem` (factTag <$> L.get rPrems ru))
129	      prem <- L.get rPrems ru
130	      guard (factTag prem == tag)
131	      guard (not (null $ factTerms conc))
132	      return (tag, combineShapes (getShape conc) (getShape prem))
133	      where
134	        -- Compute the default shape of the fact. I.e., a correct shape
135	        -- with unspecified behaviour.
136	        getShape :: LNFact -> [[MonotonicBehaviour]]
137	        getShape (factTerms -> _ : terms) = map (flip replicate Unspecified . length . getPairTerms) terms
138	        getShape _ = error "a fact without terms cannot be injective"
139	
140	        -- Combining two shapes is simply taking the shorter list at each position
141	        combineShapes :: [[MonotonicBehaviour]] -> [[MonotonicBehaviour]] -> [[MonotonicBehaviour]]
142	        combineShapes behaviours behaviours1 = map (map fst) $ zipWith zip behaviours behaviours1 -- zip automatically orients itself on the shorter list
143	
144	    combineAll :: [Maybe [[MonotonicBehaviour]]] -> FactTag -> Maybe [[MonotonicBehaviour]]
145	    combineAll list _ | any isNothing list = Nothing -- if any of the elements say that the tag is not injective, then return nothing
146	    combineAll (Just behaviours : Just behaviours1 : rest) tag =
147	      -- trace (show("combineAll", behaviours, behaviours1, (map (map combine) $ zipWith zip behaviours behaviours1))) $
148	      combineAll (Just (map (map combine) $ zipWith zip behaviours behaviours1) : rest) tag
149	    combineAll [x] _ = x
150	    combineAll [] tag = M.lookup tag candidates -- start with the empty shape of Unspecified
151	    combineAll _ _ = error "The haskell compiler is too dumb to know that the pattern matching is actually exhaustive."
152	
153	    combine :: (MonotonicBehaviour, MonotonicBehaviour) -> MonotonicBehaviour
154	    combine (x, y) | x == y = x
155	    combine (Unstable, _) = Unstable
156	    combine (_, Unstable) = Unstable
157	    combine (Unspecified, y) = y
158	    combine (x, Unspecified) = x
159	    combine (StrictlyIncreasing, y) | y `elem` [Increasing, Constant] = Increasing
160	    combine (StrictlyDecreasing, y) | y `elem` [Decreasing, Constant] = Decreasing
161	    combine (StrictlyIncreasing, _) = Unstable -- with [Strictly]Decreasing
162	    combine (StrictlyDecreasing, _) = Unstable -- with [Strictly]Increasing
163	    combine (Increasing, Decreasing) = Unstable
164	    combine (Increasing, Constant) = Increasing
165	    combine (Decreasing, Constant) = Decreasing
166	    combine (x, y) = combine (y, x)
167	
168	    -- Returns Nothing if the fact $tag$ violates injectivity guidelines in rule $ru$
169	    -- If the fact is injective, it computes the shape/behaviour of the fact.
170	    getMaybeEqStrict :: FactTag -> ProtoRuleE -> Maybe [[MonotonicBehaviour]]
171	    getMaybeEqStrict tag ru =
172	      -- trace (show ("getMaybeEqStrict", tag, ru, combineAll (map getMaybeEqMonConclusion copies) tag)) $
173	      combineAll (map getMaybeEqMonConclusion copies) tag
174	      where
175	        prems = L.get rPrems ru
176	        copies = filter ((tag ==) . factTag) (L.get rConcs ru)
177	        constraints = concatMap extractConstraints (L.get preRestriction (L.get rInfo ru))
178	        firstTerm = headMay . factTerms
179	
180	        -- duplicateFirstTerms are the first terms that appear at least twice - i.e. the corresponding fact cannot be injective
181	        allFirstTerms = sort $ mapMaybe firstTerm copies
182	        duplicateFirstTerms = S.fromList [a | (a, b) <- zip (drop 1 allFirstTerms) (take (length allFirstTerms - 1) allFirstTerms), a == b]
183	
184	        -- For a given fact, computes whether it is injective and, if so, computes its shape/behaviour in the rule
185	        getMaybeEqMonConclusion :: LNFact -> Maybe [[MonotonicBehaviour]]
186	        getMaybeEqMonConclusion faConc = case firstTerm faConc of
187	          Nothing -> Nothing -- cannot be an injective fact if it has no arguments
188	          Just tConc | tConc `S.member` duplicateFirstTerms -> Nothing -- violating (2)
189	          Just tConc | freshFact tConc `elem` prems -> M.lookup tag candidates -- applying (2)(a)
190	          Just tConc -> case getPrem tConc of
191	            Nothing -> Nothing -- violating (2)(b)
192	            Just faPrem -> Just behaviours
193	              where
194	                -- The default shape for this candidate
195	                shape = fromJust $ M.lookup tag candidates
196	
197	                -- Unfold the tuple to the right N -1 times.
198	                shapeTerm :: LNTerm -> Int -> [LNTerm]
199	                shapeTerm (viewTerm2 -> FPair t1 t2) x | x > 1 = t1 : shapeTerm t2 (x - 1)
200	                shapeTerm _ x | x > 1 = error "shapeTerm: the term does not have enough pairs"
201	                shapeTerm t x | x == 1 = [t]
202	                shapeTerm _ _ = error "shapeTerm: cannot take an integer with size less than 1"
203	
204	                -- Unfold each term according to the default shape
205	                trimmedPairTerms :: LNFact -> [[LNTerm]]
206	                trimmedPairTerms (factTerms -> _ : terms) = zipWith shapeTerm terms (map length shape)
207	                trimmedPairTerms _ = error "a fact with no terms cannot be injective"
208	
209	                -- Zip the matching terms from premise instance and conclusion instance
210	                zipped = zipWith zip (trimmedPairTerms faPrem) (trimmedPairTerms faConc)
211	
212	                -- Compute the behaviour for two matching terms as computed by zip
213	                getBehaviour :: (LNTerm, LNTerm) -> MonotonicBehaviour
214	                getBehaviour (t1, t2) | t1 == t2 = Constant
215	                getBehaviour (t1, t2) | elemNotBelowReducible reducible t1 t2 = StrictlyIncreasing
216	                getBehaviour (t1, t2) | elemNotBelowReducible reducible t2 t1 = StrictlyDecreasing
217	                getBehaviour (t1, t2) | (t1,t2) `elem` constraints = StrictlyIncreasing
218	                getBehaviour (t1, t2) | (t2,t1) `elem` constraints = StrictlyDecreasing
219	                getBehaviour _ = Unstable
220	
221	                behaviours =
222	                  -- trace (show("zipped,final", zipped, map (map getBehaviour) zipped)) $
223	                  map (map getBehaviour) zipped
224	
225	        -- get the corresponding fact in the premise
226	        getPrem tConc = case (`filter` prems) (\faPrem -> factTag faPrem == tag && Just tConc == firstTerm faPrem) of
227	          [g] -> Just g
228	          _ -> Nothing -- if there are multiple such guards, the rule cannot be executed
229
