1	module Theory.Tools.MessageDerivationChecks (
2	      checkVariableDeducability
3	    , diffCheckVariableDeducability
4	) where
5	
6	import  Theory.Model.Formula
7	import  qualified Data.Label as L
8	import Items.RuleItem
9	import TheoryObject
10	import Theory.Model
11	import Theory.Proof
12	import Prover
13	import Data.Maybe ( fromJust, fromMaybe, catMaybes, mapMaybe )
14	import Data.List
15	import Theory.Tools.Wellformedness (WfErrorReport, underlineTopic)
16	import Text.PrettyPrint.Class
17	import qualified Text.PrettyPrint.Class as Pretty
18	import ClosedTheory
19	import qualified Data.List as List
20	
21	import Control.Basics
22	import Control.Category
23	
24	import Prelude hiding (id, (.))
25	
26	import           Prelude                             hiding (id, (.))
27	import OpenTheory
28	
29	
30	-----------------------------------------------
31	-- DerivationChecks
32	-----------------------------------------------
33	
34	
35	checkVariableDeducability :: OpenTranslatedTheory -> SignatureWithMaude -> Bool -> Prover -> WfErrorReport
36	checkVariableDeducability thy sig sources prover =
37	    reportVars (map checkProofStatuses provenTheories) originalRules freeVars
38	    where
39	        originalRules = map (applyMacroInProtoRule (theoryMacros thy)) $ theoryRules thy
40	        provenTheories =  map (proveTheory (const True) prover) closedTheories
41	        closedTheories = map (\t -> closeTheoryWithMaude sig t sources False) modifiedTheories
42	        modifiedTheories =  zipWith3 (\r l t -> (addRules [r] . addLemmas l ) t)  newRules newLemmas (repeat emptyPublicThy)
43	        emptyPublicThy = makeFunsPublic (toSignaturePure sig) $ deleteRulesAndLemmasAndRestrictionsFromTheory thy
44	        newRules = zipWith3 (\idx freevs prems -> generateRule freevs (premisesToOut prems) idx) [0..] freeVars premises
45	        newLemmas = zipWith3 (\idx freevs _-> generateSeparatedLemmas idx freevs) [0..] freeVars premises
46	        premises = map (map (fmap replacePrivate)) $ premsOfThyRules originalRules
47	        freeVars = freesInThyRules originalRules
48	
49	diffCheckVariableDeducability :: OpenDiffTheory -> SignatureWithMaude -> Bool -> Prover -> DiffProver -> WfErrorReport
50	diffCheckVariableDeducability thy sig sources prover diffprover =
51	    reportDiffVars (map checkDiffProofStatuses provenTheories) originalRules freeVars
52	    where
53	        originalRules = diffTheoryDiffRules thy
54	        provenTheories =  map (proveDiffTheory (const True) prover diffprover) closedTheories
55	        closedTheories = map (\t -> closeDiffTheoryWithMaude sig t sources) modifiedTheories
56	        modifiedTheories =  map (\(r,l,t) -> (addDiffRules [r] . addDiffLemmas l ) t) (zip3 newrules newlemmas (repeat emptyPublicThy))
57	        emptyPublicThy = diffmakeFunsPublic (toSignaturePure sig) $ diffdeleteRulesAndLemmasAndRestrictionsFromTheory thy
58	        newrules =  map (\(idx, freevs, prems )-> generateRule freevs (premisesToOut prems) idx) freesAndPrems
59	        newlemmas =  map (\(idx, freevs, _) -> generateSeparatedLemmas idx freevs) freesAndPrems
60	        freesAndPrems = freesAndPremsLHS ++ freesAndPremsRHS
61	        freesAndPremsRHS = zip3 [0..] (freesInThyRules rightOpenRules) (map (map (fmap replacePrivate)) $ premsOfThyRules rightOpenRules)
62	        freesAndPremsLHS = zip3 [0..] (freesInThyRules leftOpenRules) (map (map (fmap replacePrivate)) $ premsOfThyRules leftOpenRules)
63	        freeVars = freesInThyRules (leftOpenRules ++ rightOpenRules)
64	
65	        diffRules  = map (applyMacroInDiffProtoRule (diffTheoryMacros thy)) $ diffTheoryDiffRules thy
66	        leftOpenRules  = map getLeftProtoRule  diffRules
67	        rightOpenRules = map getRightProtoRule diffRules
68	
69	
70	-----------------------------------------------
71	-- Manipulating Theories
72	-----------------------------------------------
73	
74	diffdeleteRulesAndLemmasAndRestrictionsFromTheory :: DiffTheory sig c r r2 p p2 -> DiffTheory sig c r r2 p p2
75	diffdeleteRulesAndLemmasAndRestrictionsFromTheory = L.modify diffThyItems deleteDiffRules
76	    where
77	        deleteDiffRules = mapMaybe delRules
78	        delRules (DiffRuleItem _) = Nothing
79	        delRules (EitherRuleItem _) = Nothing
80	        delRules (DiffLemmaItem _ ) = Nothing
81	        delRules (EitherLemmaItem _) = Nothing
82	        delRules (EitherRestrictionItem _) = Nothing
83	        delRules sth = Just sth
84	
85	deleteRulesAndLemmasAndRestrictionsFromTheory :: Theory sig c r p s -> Theory sig c r p s
86	deleteRulesAndLemmasAndRestrictionsFromTheory = L.modify thyItems deleteRules
87	    where
88	        deleteRules = mapMaybe delRules
89	        delRules (RuleItem _) = Nothing
90	        delRules (LemmaItem _) = Nothing
91	        delRules (RestrictionItem _) = Nothing
92	        delRules sth = Just sth
93	
94	replacePrivate :: Term t -> Term t
95	replacePrivate t = case viewTerm t of
96	    FApp (NoEq (num,(name,Private,constr))) term  -> termViewToTerm $ FApp (NoEq (num, (name, Public, constr))) (map replacePrivate term)
97	    FApp sym as -> termViewToTerm $ FApp sym (map replacePrivate as)
98	    x -> termViewToTerm x
99	
100	makeFunsPublic ::  sig -> Theory sig c r p s  -> Theory sig c r p s
101	makeFunsPublic = L.set thySignature
102	
103	diffmakeFunsPublic :: a -> DiffTheory a c r r2 p p2 -> DiffTheory a c r r2 p p2
104	diffmakeFunsPublic = L.set diffThySignature
105	
106	addLemmas :: Foldable t =>t (Lemma p) -> Theory sig c r p s -> Theory sig c r p s
107	addLemmas lemmas thy = fromMaybe thy $ foldl ( \fm lemma -> addLemma lemma (fromJust fm)) (Just thy) lemmas
108	
109	addDiffRules :: [OpenProtoRule] -> DiffTheory sig c DiffProtoRule r2 p p2-> DiffTheory sig c DiffProtoRule r2 p p2
110	addDiffRules rules = L.modify diffThyItems (++ map (DiffRuleItem . (\t -> DiffProtoRule (L.get oprRuleE t) (Just (t,t)))) rules)
111	
112	addRules :: [r] -> Theory sig c r p s -> Theory sig c r p s
113	addRules rules = L.modify thyItems (++ map RuleItem rules)
114	
115	addDiffLemmas :: Foldable t =>t (Lemma p2)-> DiffTheory sig c r r2 p p2 -> DiffTheory sig c r r2 p p2
116	addDiffLemmas lemmas thy = fromMaybe thy $ foldl ( \fm lemma ->  addLemmaDiff LHS lemma (fromJust fm)) (Just thy) lemmas
117	
118	-----------------------------------------------
119	-- Generating error reports
120	-----------------------------------------------
121	
122	reportVars :: [[ProofStatus]] -> [OpenProtoRule] -> [[LVar]] -> WfErrorReport
123	reportVars analysisresults rules vars = case rulesAndVars of
124	    []     -> []
125	    errors -> [(underlineTopic "Message Derivation Checks",
126	        text $ "The variables of the following rule(s) are not derivable from their premises, you may be performing unintended pattern matching.\n\n"
127	        ++ errors)]
128	    where
129	        rulesAndVars :: String
130	        rulesAndVars = intercalate "\n\n" $ catMaybes (zipWith3 (\results rule vars' ->
131	            if List.any ( /= TraceFound) results && not (ignoreDerivChecks (ruleAttributes $ L.get oprRuleE rule))
132	                then Just $ generateError results rule vars'
133	                else Nothing)
134	            analysisresults rules vars)
135	
136	        generateError :: [ProofStatus] -> OpenProtoRule -> [LVar] -> String
137	        generateError results rule vars' = "Rule " ++ (Pretty.render . prettyProtoRuleName) (L.get preName (L.get rInfo (L.get oprRuleE rule)))
138	                ++ ": \nFailed to derive Variable(s): " ++ intercalate ", " (map (show . snd) $ filter ((/= TraceFound) . fst) (zip results vars'))
139	
140	reportDiffVars :: [[ProofStatus]] -> [DiffProtoRule] -> [[LVar]] -> WfErrorReport
141	reportDiffVars analysisresults rules vars = case rulesAndVars of
142	    []     -> []
143	    errors -> [(underlineTopic "Message Derivation Checks",
144	        text $ "The variables of the following rule(s) are not derivable from their premises, you may be performing unintended pattern matching.\n\n"
145	        ++ errors)]
146	    where
147	        rulesAndVars :: String
148	        rulesAndVars = intercalate "\n\n" $ catMaybes (zipWith3 (\results rule vars' ->
149	            if List.any ( /= TraceFound) results && not (ignoreDerivChecks (ruleAttributes $ L.get dprRule rule))
150	                then Just $ generateError results rule vars'
151	                else Nothing)
152	            analysisresults rules vars)
153	
154	        generateError :: [ProofStatus] -> DiffProtoRule -> [LVar] -> String
155	        generateError results rule vars' = "Rule " ++ (Pretty.render . prettyProtoRuleName) (L.get preName (L.get rInfo (L.get dprRule rule)))
156	                ++ ": \nFailed to derive Variable(s): " ++ intercalate ", " (map (show . snd) $ filter ((/= TraceFound) . fst) (zip results vars'))
157	
158	checkProofStatuses :: ClosedTheory -> [ProofStatus]
159	checkProofStatuses thy =  map (foldProof proofStepStatus . L.get lProof) $ theoryLemmas thy
160	
161	checkDiffProofStatuses :: ClosedDiffTheory -> [ProofStatus]
162	checkDiffProofStatuses thy = map (foldProof proofStepStatus . L.get lProof . snd) $ diffTheoryLemmas thy
163	
164	-----------------------------------------------
165	-- Convenience getter functions
166	-----------------------------------------------
167	
168	freesInThyRules :: [OpenProtoRule] -> [[LVar]]
169	freesInThyRules =
170	    -- Timepoint variables such as #NOW come from _restrict annotations but
171	    -- are not message variables we need to prove deducible.
172	    map (filter ((/= LSortNode) . lvarSort) . frees . L.get oprRuleE)
173	
174	premsOfThyRules :: [OpenProtoRule] -> [[LNFact]]
175	premsOfThyRules = map (L.get rPrems . L.get oprRuleE)
176	
177	----------------------------------------------------
178	-- Helper functions for rule and lemma generation
179	----------------------------------------------------
180	
181	generateRule ::  [LVar] -> [LNFact] -> Int -> OpenProtoRule
182	generateRule freevars premises idx =  OpenProtoRule (Rule (ProtoRuleEInfo (StandRule (show idx)) mempty []) (freesToFresh . deleteGlobals $ freevars) (premisesToOut premises ) ([generateAction (deleteGlobals freevars) idx]) ([])) []
183	
184	generateAction :: [LVar] ->Int -> LNFact
185	generateAction vars idx = protoFact Persistent ("Generated_" ++ show idx) (map lvarToLnterm (deleteGlobals vars))
186	
187	generateSeparatedLemmas :: Int -> [LVar]-> [ProtoLemma (ProtoFormula Unit2 (String, LSort) Name LVar) (Proof ())]
188	generateSeparatedLemmas idx vars = map (\v -> Lemma (show v) "message_deriv" False ExistsTrace (existsTimeFormula $ existFormula $ landFormula $ generateAction vars idx : [(lntermToKUFact (lvarToLnterm v))]) Nothing [] (unproven ())) vars
189	
190	deleteGlobals :: [LVar] -> [LVar]
191	deleteGlobals = filter (\v -> lvarSort v /= LSortPub)
192	
193	-- Exists-quantifies every non-time LVar of a formula
194	existFormula ::  ProtoFormula Unit2 (String,LSort) Name LVar -> ProtoFormula Unit2 (String,LSort) Name LVar
195	existFormula fm = foldl (\ formula var -> exists (lvarName var, lvarSort var) var formula) fm (frees fm)
196	
197	-- Assumes function passed only has free time variables anymore and exists-quantifies them
198	existsTimeFormula :: ProtoFormula Unit2 (String,LSort) Name LVar -> ProtoFormula Unit2 (String,LSort) Name LVar
199	existsTimeFormula fm = foldl (\ formula var -> exists (lvarName var, LSortNode) var formula) fm (frees fm)
200	
201	-- Takes a list of facts and logically ands them into a formula that can be used for a lemma
202	landFormula :: [LNFact] -> ProtoFormula Unit2 (String,LSort) Name  LVar
203	landFormula facts = foldl (\ fm (idx, fact) -> fm .&&. Ato (Action (LIT (Var (Free (LVar (show (idx :: Integer)) LSortNode 0))) ) fact ))  ltrue (zip [0..]  (map freeFact facts))
204	
205	-- Transforms different kind of facts into the desired form
206	freesToFresh :: [LVar] -> [LNFact]
207	freesToFresh = map (freshFact . lvarToLnterm)
208	
209	premisesToOut :: [LNFact] -> [LNFact]
210	premisesToOut =  map (outFact . natToFreshVars) . concatMap factTerms
211	
212	
213	freeFact :: LNFact ->  Fact (Term (Lit Name (BVar LVar)))
214	freeFact = fmap freeTerm
215	
216	lvarToLnterm :: LVar -> LNTerm
217	lvarToLnterm (LVar name LSortNat idx) = LIT $ Var $ LVar name LSortFresh idx
218	lvarToLnterm v                        = LIT $ Var v
219	
220	lntermToKUFact :: LNTerm -> LNFact
221	lntermToKUFact = kuFact

1	# BEHAVIOR.md — Unit G: Message Derivation Checks
2	
3	All statements below are derived from black-box oracle observation (see QUERIES.log),
4	the captured example outputs, or the SPEC. No tamarin-prover source was read.
5	
6	## 1. What the check is
7	
8	For each protocol rule, the prover's solver decides whether every variable of the rule is
9	**derivable by the intruder from the rule's premises**. Variables that are NOT derivable are
10	reported under the wellformedness topic `Message Derivation Checks`, warning that the rule may
11	be "performing unintended pattern matching" on structure the adversary cannot freely produce.
12	
13	The derivability decision itself is the prover's solver and is NOT reimplemented here — it is a
14	caller-supplied callback (see §7). This unit owns: rule/variable selection (probe construction),
15	the decision logic around callback outcomes, and byte-exact report text.
16	
17	## 2. Report position and topic
18	
19	- Topic string (exact): `Message Derivation Checks`.
20	- Underline: a run of `=` exactly as long as the title (25 chars).
21	- Order among wellformedness topics: emitted **last**, after `Subterm Convergence Warning`
22	  (observed order: "Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side" →
23	  "Subterm Convergence Warning" → "Message Derivation Checks").
24	- Driver phase-log lines (NOT part of the report): `[Theory <name>] Derivation checks started`
25	  and `... ended`. In `--diff` mode intermediate `[Saturating Sources]` lines appear between
26	  them. These are emitted by the driver around the check; this unit does not render them.
27	
28	## 3. Byte-exact block format
29	
30	Single failing rule:
31	```
32	Message Derivation Checks
33	=========================
34	
35	  The variables of the following rule(s) are not derivable from their premises, you may be performing unintended pattern matching.
36	
37	Rule R: 
38	Failed to derive Variable(s): w
39	```
40	Two failing rules (uniform blank-line separation between intro and each rule block):
41	```
42	Message Derivation Checks
43	=========================
44	
45	  The variables of the following rule(s) are not derivable from their premises, you may be performing unintended pattern matching.
46	
47	Rule reSign: 
48	Failed to derive Variable(s): m, r2, sk2
49	
50	Rule RP_gets_idToken: 
51	Failed to derive Variable(s): pkA
52	```
53	Exact pieces:
54	- Header: `"Message Derivation Checks\n" + "="*25 + "\n"`.
55	- Blank line after header.
56	- Intro (2-space indent, no trailing space):
57	  `"  The variables of the following rule(s) are not derivable from their premises, you may be performing unintended pattern matching."`
58	- One block per rule: `"Rule " + name + ": \n"` (NOTE the trailing space after the colon)
59	  then `"Failed to derive Variable(s): " + vars` where `vars` is the sorted list joined by `", "`.
60	- Header, intro, and each rule block are separated by a single blank line, i.e. the message
61	  segments `[intro, rule1, rule2, …]` are joined by `"\n\n"` and appended after `header + "\n"`.
62	
63	## 4. Which RULES are probed / reported
64	
65	- Only theory `Rule` items (protocol rules); intruder rules are not user rules and are out of scope.
66	- A rule carrying the `[no_derivcheck]` attribute (`RuleAttr::NoDerivCheck`) is skipped entirely.
67	- Rules are reported in **theory (source) order** (o3: `Zebra` before `Apple`).
68	- A rule with zero non-derivable variables produces no block (s4: `Good` omitted).
69	- Rule name is printed verbatim (case preserved), without any `(modulo E)` qualifier.
70	- If no rule has a non-derivable variable, the topic is absent entirely.
71	
72	## 5. Which VARIABLES are reported (solver-decided; observed semantics)
73	
74	The candidate set this unit hands the callback is the set of variables occurring syntactically in
75	the rule; the callback returns derivability. Observed callback verdicts (NOT reimplemented here):
76	- bare variable in `In(x)` → derivable (p1).
77	- variable inside a one-way / private function application in `In(h(x))`, `In(skf(x))` →
78	  NOT derivable (p6, d2).
79	- variables inside an invertible pair `In(<a,b>)` → derivable (p4).
80	- fresh variable bound by a `Fr()` premise → derivable (p3).
81	- fresh variable occurring only inside an `In` term `In(h(~n))` → NOT derivable (o2).
82	- public variable `$p` → always derivable, never flagged (o2).
83	- variable bound by a non-`In` state-fact premise `St(x)` → derivable (s3).
84	- a variable occurring multiple times is listed once (d1: de-duplicated).
85	
86	## 6. Variable ordering (this unit's responsibility)
87	
88	Reported ascending by the key `(sort_rank, name, index)`:
89	1. `sort_rank`: observed `Fresh < Msg` (s1: `~zzz` before `aaa`; o2: `~n` before `x`).
90	   Public is excluded (never a candidate result); Nat/Node do not occur as message-derivable
91	   candidates. Sort dominates name (s1 proves this).
92	2. `name`: **lexicographic string** comparison (o1: `v1 < v10 < v2`; `aa < mm < zz`;
93	   `m < r2 < sk2`).
94	3. `index`: **numeric** comparison (s2: `x.1 < x.2 < x.10`).
95	Rendering of each variable: fresh → `~name`, message → `name`; a nonzero index appends `.idx`
96	(e.g. `x.2`). This matches the pretty-printed variable spelling used in the warning.
97	
98	## 7. Activation / timeout
99	
100	- Controlled by `--derivcheck-timeout=INT` seconds (default 5). The optional argument REQUIRES
101	  `=`; `-d 1` is mis-parsed (the `1` becomes a file argument).
102	- `=0` fully DEACTIVATES the check: the `Message Derivation Checks` topic is absent; other wf
103	  topics are unaffected; the driver phase-log lines still print. In this unit, a timeout budget
104	  of 0 ⇒ empty report, short-circuiting before any callback.
105	- `=1..5`: the check runs; on all sane inputs the derivation computation is fast (even an 8-term
106	  xor input finishes the check quickly — the observed slowness there is later precomputation,
107	  not the check). A per-rule timeout could therefore not be forced with practical inputs.
108	
109	### Residual (unobserved): per-rule timeout output text
110	Because the check is robustly fast, the exact behavior when a single rule's derivation check
111	exceeds the budget was not observable. The callback trait exposes a `TimedOut` outcome and this
112	unit's decision logic treats it **fail-open**: a timed-out variable/rule yields NO warning for
113	that rule. Rationale: the only timeout-related observation (`=0` ⇒ no warnings at all) shows the
114	tool suppresses output when the check cannot complete. This is documented as a residual; the
115	alternative policy (emit the partial/timed-out rule) is isolated to one match arm and one flag.
116	
117	## 8. Count interaction (informational)
118	
119	The driver's `WARNING: N wellformedness check failed!` counts distinct failing TOPICS
120	(POIDC_CMB: Subterm + Derivation = 2; OIDC_CodeFlow: LHS-facts + Subterm + Derivation = 3).
121	This whole topic contributes 1, regardless of how many rules/variables it lists. Counting is the
122	wellformedness driver's concern, not this unit's.
123

# Similarity audit — macros / macro-clean

## Round 1 audit

Reviewer: similarity auditor (both-sides). Compared
`workspace/macro-clean/src/{lib.rs,ast.rs}` against HASKELL
`lib/term/src/Term/Macro.hs`, the macro handling threaded through
`lib/theory/src/TheoryObject.hs` and the per-item `applyMacroIn*` functions
(`Theory/Model/{Rule,Fact,Formula,Restriction}.hs`, `Lemma.hs`,
`ClosedTheory.hs`), and `lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Parser/Macro.hs`.

**Findings: none (0).**

The core algorithm is materially different, not a mirror:

- Architecture. Haskell has NO single whole-theory expansion pass; macro
  application is scattered and lazy — `applyMacroInRule`, `applyMacroInFormula`
  (via generic `mapAtoms`), `applyMacroInLemma`, `applyMacroInFact`, etc., each
  invoked at different pipeline stages (rule closing, proving, wellformedness,
  export) over a flat unexpanded `[Macro]` list. The clean unit implements a
  single eager `expand(theory) -> theory` (lib.rs:44) that walks the clean AST
  once and drops the declarations — the design the SPEC mandates. The traversal
  (`expand_item`/`expand_rule`/`expand_formula`/…) is dictated by the vendored
  clean AST (ast.rs), so visiting every term-bearing field is forced, not copied.
- Term expansion. Haskell `applyMacros` (Macro.hs:40) keeps bodies unexpanded,
  matches a funsym by name+arity (`macroToFunSym`), and RE-RUNS `applyMacros` on
  the substituted body to expand nested calls. The clean unit instead
  pre-expands every body into a DAG-ordered `MacroTable` (`build_table`,
  lib.rs:65) keyed by name, then does a single `substitute_term` with NO re-run
  (lib.rs:82,141). Different accounting (memoized table + separate substitution
  vs. flat-list lazy re-expansion).

Shared shape that is filtered out: "expand args, then substitute formals→args
into the body" is the only correct eager, capture-avoiding, parallel-substitution
order, dictated by the observed macro semantics ([Q7]); the load-bearing
structural choice (pre-expand vs. re-run) differs.

Verdict: pass.

# Similarity audit — derivcheck / derivcheck-clean

## Round 1 audit

Reviewer: similarity auditor (both-sides). Compared
`workspace/derivcheck-clean/src/lib.rs` against HASKELL
`lib/theory/src/Theory/Tools/MessageDerivationChecks.hs`
(`checkVariableDeducability` and helpers). Key question from the brief: the
callback-trait architecture must NOT mirror the Haskell probe-theory
construction; probe payloads must be behaviorally motivated. Cross-checked
against `workspace/QUERIES.log` and `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md`.

**Findings: none (0). The callback trait does not mirror the probe-theory
construction.**

- Probe architecture. Haskell decides derivability by CONSTRUCTING an auxiliary
  provable theory per rule: strip rules/lemmas/restrictions, make funs public
  (`deleteRulesAndLemmasAndRestrictionsFromTheory`, `makeFunsPublic`), turn
  premises into `Out` facts (`premisesToOut`), synthesize a rule
  (`generateRule`) and one exists-trace `KU` lemma per free var
  (`generateSeparatedLemmas`), close with maude and prove
  (MessageDerivationChecks.hs:36–47, 181–221). The clean unit reproduces NONE of
  this: it defines a `DerivabilitySolver` trait and hands the solver a
  `DerivProbe { rule_name, rule, variable, premises, timeout_secs }` (lib.rs:56–79)
  — the direct restatement of the observable check ("variables … not derivable
  from their premises", BEHAVIOR.md §1/§5). The payload carries the abstract
  question, not the Haskell's Out-fact/KU-lemma encoding; the derivability oracle
  is explicitly delegated to the caller and not reimplemented. Behaviorally
  motivated → survives the mirror charge as a genuinely different design.
- Report text and ordering. The identical strings (topic `Message Derivation
  Checks`, 25-`=` underline, 2-space intro paragraph, `"Rule <name>: \n"` with
  trailing space, `"Failed to derive Variable(s): "`) are verbatim observed
  output (BEHAVIOR.md §3 cat -A captures; QUERIES.log entry 3) — compatibility
  content, filtered out. The sort key `(sort_rank Fresh<Msg, name lexicographic,
  idx numeric)` was probed (o1/o2/s1/s2) and the clean re-sorts explicitly, where
  Haskell relies on `frees` order — different mechanism.
- Report model. Clean emits multiple `WfError` sharing a topic, joined at render
  (lib.rs:180–186, 332–346); Haskell emits a single concatenated-body tuple
  (reportVars:122–138). Different decomposition.
- Timeout policy (`TimeoutPolicy`, `timeout_secs==0` deactivation) is the clean's
  own design over an unobserved residual (BEHAVIOR.md §7), not a Haskell mirror.

Verdict: pass.
